Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Mar-14-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Sean Novak, Esq. 323-424-4313 of the Novak Law Firm for Plaintiff Roger Senders through Court Call.
Elaisha Nandrajog, Esq. 650-460-1528 of Sacks Ricketts & Case for Defendant AIRBNB, INC.

RULING - The matter is Discussed. Party seeks clarification on the tentative ruling. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling with an addition to the stay as to AIRBNB only and not to Mr. Burland as follows:

DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration.

Defendant's Airbnb, Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted and this case is stayed as to AIRBNB only pending the conclusion of arbitration proceedings regarding the claims alleged in the complaint filed by plaintiff Roger Senders. Airbnb has shown that Mr. Senders assented to the Terms of Service, including the arbitration language therein, because the sign up screen adequately informed Mr. Senders that, by clicking to sign up, he was agreeing to the Terms of Service. The notice "by signing up, I agree to" is placed in close proximity to the three boxes where a consumer clicks to sign up, the text is in bold letters, and the hyperlinked text "Terms of Service" immediately follows. The arbitration language is clear and unmistakable evidence of the parties' intent to delegate issues of arbitrability, including whether the arbitration agreement is unconscionable, to the arbitrator. (Aanderud v. Superior Court (2017) 13 Cal.App.5th 880, 892). Mr. Senders' citation to older case law precluding delegation of arbitrability to an arbitrator is misplaced because the more recent cases unequivocally hold that such delegation is not per se impermissible. The delegation clause is not unconscionable and is enforceable.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/HK)
